resources.string: A premium prototype for controlled election access, QR-based voter check-in, transparent blockchain vote recording, and receipt verification.
Create and manage elections, verify eligible voters, complete QR check-in, monitor turnout, and access results securely.
Access the voter flow, present the wallet QR for polling check-in, cast a vote when eligible, and verify the receipt afterward.
• Admin users continue through a protected management portal.
• Voters continue through a separate access path for QR check-in and voting.
• Election actions are designed to support transparency, auditability, and controlled access.
Direct dashboard shortcuts have been removed so each user must continue through the correct access path.
Protected access for election setup, voter check-in management, turnout visibility, and results control.
Only authorized election administrators or polling officers should continue. This prototype uses a controlled access code before supervised admin wallet actions are enabled.
Required to unlock the protected admin control portal for this prototype.
Enter the admin access code to continue.
Incorrect admin access code. Please try again.
• Use this portal only for authorized election administration tasks.
• Voter access remains separated through its own portal and check-in flow.
• Blockchain-backed actions support transparency and auditability in this supervised prototype workflow.
In a production deployment, this step would be replaced by secure backend authentication, protected credential handling, and stronger role verification.
This portal guides eligible voters through supervised QR-based check-in, controlled vote access, and receipt verification after submission.
Voter QR for Polling Officer Check-In
Show this QR code to the polling officer during check-in. It contains the voter wallet address used for whitelist matching and supervised access to the voting flow.
For controlled single-device testing, manual wallet entry in the admin QR Check-In screen can still be used as a fallback.
Continue to the voter portal to review eligible elections, cast a vote when permitted, and verify the receipt after blockchain recording is completed.
• Voting access still depends on whitelist eligibility, QR check-in status, and election timing rules.
• Only checked-in eligible voters should be able to continue through the final voting workflow.
• Successful vote submission should produce a receipt and transaction hash for later verification and audit review.
Prototype voter wallet mode is currently enabled for supervised local testing.
Set up the election details, candidate list, schedule, and eligible voter whitelist.

Enter candidate names separated by commas or new lines
Example:
Alice
Bob
Charlie

Enter wallet addresses separated by commas or new lines
Example:
0x70997970c51812dc3a010c7d01b50e0d17dc79c8
0x3c44cdddb6a900fa2b585dd299e03d12fa4293bc
Use Ethereum wallet addresses for the whitelist.\nUse this date/time format: dd/MM/yyyy hh:mm a\nExample: 29/03/2026 08:30 PM
Please enter at least 1 eligible voter wallet address.
Invalid start date/time. Use format: dd/MM/yyyy hh:mm a
Invalid end date/time. Use format: dd/MM/yyyy hh:mm a
End time must be later than start time.
Failed to create election on blockchain.
The election has been created and prepared for eligible voters.
Manage elections, complete QR check-in, monitor turnout, and review blockchain-backed election activity.
Review active elections, vote after polling-officer check-in, and verify your blockchain receipt.
Review created elections, status, turnout, and schedule.
Review election timing and status before you proceed to vote.
Core administration tasks for election setup and monitoring.
Proceed to cast your vote or verify your blockchain receipt.
Create your first election to begin whitelist setup, QR check-in, voting, and receipt tracking.
When an election becomes available, the polling officer will check you in before you vote and verify your receipt.
